seoTitle: SaaS MVP: feasibility, stack, and phasing before big spend | Luc Rousseau
seoDescription: New SaaS or MVP: framing, stack choice, prototypes, and prerequisites before agency or team commit — phased mandate, no external tech lead.

hero.title: Launching a SaaS or MVP: frame before building big
hero.lede:
You have an idea, a market, sometimes budget or an agency in sight — but no clear map yet: stack, first-release scope, risks, what to validate before months of dev.

I work in phased mandates: feasibility, go/no-go, defensible architecture, prototypes (including AI) to test hypotheses — then handover to your devs or agency. Not a CTO who takes over your team: someone who structures, challenges, and can ship on high-leverage topics.

blocks.title: Build too early, validate too late
blocks.lede: Without framing, v1 drifts: scope swells, default stack, agency or devs coding while what and why still move. Fixing after three months of build costs far more than a few weeks of feasibility.

items.title: Phase 1 — Feasibility and direction
items.content: Hypotheses, constraints, technical options, ballpark, go/no-go — deliverable: documented decision, not a vague deck.

items.title: Phase 2 — Architecture and first scope
items.content: Boundaries, APIs, stack (Laravel, React/Next, headless if relevant), realistic MVP: what's in v1 and what waits.

items.title: Phase 3 — Prototypes and prerequisites
items.content: Clickable flows, light AI or technical POCs, acceptance criteria — so agency or team builds without ambiguity.

items.title: Optional follow-up
items.content: After major framing, lighter rhythm to keep coherence during build — without a full-time role.

blocks.title: Build straight with agency/devs vs product-system framing first
blocks.intro: Complements the « first dev » guide when the stake is the SaaS product, not only first tech hire.

headers.buildFirst: Agency brief or first devs immediately

rows.phaseFirst: v1 scope and hypotheses tested before big commit.

rows.buildFirst: Often agency default or dev preference.
rows.phaseFirst: Choice tied to product and operations — documented.
Prototypes and users early — AI where it makes sense.
Same person from direction to prerequisites — not team manager.

items.title: We already have an agency that can do everything
items.content: Even better if they get a solid brief and prerequisites. I stay on the client side: direction, architecture, criteria — the agency builds the SaaS; I don't replace their team.

items.title: How long before we « really » code?
items.content: Often a few weeks of framing for more predictable months of build. On high-leverage topics I can code a POC — not take every ticket for you.

items.title: What if we raise after?
items.content: You arrive with plan, stack, and validation proof — less embarrassing technical due diligence. I can help sharpen the first tech or product hire profile.

blocks.label: Discuss your MVP or SaaS
blocks.teaser: SaaS idea without a clear map? One call to see if a phased mandate (feasibility → architecture → prerequisites) avoids premature build.
